behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1: f_wiring=yes (evidence: "cells A1 through A10" requires range resolution + evaluator handoff), f_real_infra=no, f_model_judgment=no, f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 mapping: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1..A10 to known values, evaluate `=SUM(A1:A10)`, assert exact numeric result and no error.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1: f_wiring=yes (evidence: "referencing another cell that contains a formula" implies dependency/evaluation chaining), f_real_infra=no, f_model_judgment=no, f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 mapping: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1, B1=`=A1*2`, C1=`=B1+1`; recalc and assert C1 equals `(A1*2)+1`.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1: f_wiring=yes (evidence: "A1 references B1, B1 references A1" needs dependency-graph cycle detection), f_real_infra=no, f_model_judgment=no, f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 mapping: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Create A1=`=B1` and B1=`=A1`; run evaluation and assert circular-reference error is returned promptly.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1: f_wiring=yes (evidence: "lookup table" implies table/range addressing plus function execution), f_real_infra=no, f_model_judgment=no, f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 mapping: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Build a table range, run exact-match VLOOKUP for present and absent keys, assert returned value vs expected not-found error.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: Stage 1: f_wiring=no (primarily nested expression semantics), f_real_infra=no, f_model_judgment=no, f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 mapping: Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate nested IF expression across boundary values (<=10, 11..20, >20) and assert outputs `low|medium|high`.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1: f_wiring=no (core is CONCATENATE argument/empty-value handling semantics), f_real_infra=no, f_model_judgment=no, f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 mapping: Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate CONCATENATE cases with normal and empty inputs; assert separator placement and omission of empty-cell contributions.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1: f_wiring=yes (evidence: "different sheet" requires cross-sheet resolver + evaluator integration), f_real_infra=no, f_model_judgment=no, f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 mapping: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Create Sheet2.A1 with value, evaluate `Sheet1!B1 = Sheet2!A1+1`, assert computed result and no reference error.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1: f_wiring=yes (evidence: "all dependent formulas" + "correct topological order" implies dependency propagation orchestration), f_real_infra=no, f_model_judgment=no, f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 mapping: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency DAG, mutate an upstream cell, trigger recalc, assert downstream values and observed/update order are topologically valid.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1: f_wiring=yes (evidence: "undo" restoring "formula text" and "dependent values" crosses editor/history/calc state boundaries), f_real_infra=no, f_model_judgment=no, f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 mapping: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Edit a formula that affects dependents, capture pre-edit state, perform undo, assert formula text and all dependent cell values match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: Stage 1: f_wiring=yes, f_real_infra=yes (evidence: "to disk" and "loading it back" require filesystem I/O and real artifact serialization), f_model_judgment=no, f_multi_agentic_steps=no. Stage 2 mapping: System.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with formulas and floating-point values, save to disk, reload, assert exact formula text equality and numeric precision invariants.
